name: manufacturing_world_model
description: Time-series forecasting, anomaly detection, and change-point analysis for manufacturing sensor data. Flagship backend is Google TimesFM-2.5 (200M, 16K context, native quantile head); falls back to Chronos-2, Granite-TTM, MOMENT, or StatsForecast if unavailable. Requires 'ruptures' for changepoint analysis.
self_test: python -c "from plugins.prometheus.manufacturing_world_model.wrapper import PLUGIN; import asyncio; r=asyncio.run(PLUGIN.invoke({'mode':'self_test'})); assert r.get('valid')"

values.description: Time-series values (e.g. temperature readings in chronological order).

timestamps.description: Optional ISO-8601 timestamps aligned with values.

horizon.description: Number of future steps to predict.

backend.description: Forecasting backend to use. 'auto' tries backends in priority order and uses the first available.

mode.description: Operation mode: 'forecast' produces predictions, 'anomaly' scores historical points, 'changepoint' detects regime shifts, 'full' runs all three, 'self_test' runs built-in demo.

adapter_path.description: Optional filesystem path to a LoRA adapter produced by finetune.py. When set, the adapter is loaded on top of frozen base weights (TimesFM 2.5 only). Omit for zero-shot inference.

forecast.description: Forecast results: median, lower, upper arrays.

anomaly_scores.description: Per-point anomaly z-scores on historical data.

changepoints.description: Detected change-point indices in the input series.
